# 23andMe CEO: ‘The human genome revealed its secrets 20 years ago. It’s time to fulfill its promise of personalized health care’

By Anne Wojcicki Anne Wojcicki

By Anne Wojcicki Anne Wojcicki

April 25, 2023, 6:17 AM ET Add us on

Anne Wojcicki is the co-founder and CEO of 23andme. Jordan Vonderhaar—Bloomberg/Getty Images

Sequencing the human genome for the first time was an incredible feat. Twenty years ago this week, the sequencing project was completed with the promise that it would transform how we predict, prevent, and treat almost all human disease.

With such an important discovery under our belt, one would believe we are now on the cusp of a new phase in personalized healthcare, one that uses genetics to inform every patient journey. Yet, we are still far from the delivery of personalized healthcare for everyone. The problem is not a lack of scientific progress, but rather a failure to implement widespread genetic testing and personalize
